choice: Treat sync as atomic-per-account and non-destructive: an expired/invalid token surfaces a 'Reconnect E*TRADE' prompt and changes nothing; an API/timeout error leaves all prior observations untouched and shows the last-successful-sync timestamp; a partial return commits only the accounts that succeeded and flags the rest as stale. Never let a failed sync zero out or overwrite existing balances.
rationale: Because precedence (777f97e5) makes sync the authoritative tier, a half-applied or empty sync response could wrongly supersede good manual/CSV data or zero real holdings. Failing closed (commit nothing on error, commit per-account on partial success) preserves the keep-every-row guarantee and keeps net-worth actuals trustworthy. Showing last-successful-sync age tells the user when the authoritative number is stale so they can fall back to manual.
